mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% in September 2010 reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 3% signals accommodative monetary policy stance that reduces equity risk premium and lowers discount rates for future cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.63% in September 2010 indicates sustained accommodation
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 3%, S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with increasing housing starts signals robust aggregate demand and consumer willingness to spend, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 339,299M and HOUST at 2,195K in August-September 2010
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with synchronized retail and housing strength
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST growth conditions are met, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rising above trend indicates strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side productivity gains, which support profit margins and equity valuations over a 20-day forward period.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 118.527 in September 2010 shows post-recession recovery
regime_hint: supply-side recovery with accelerating industrial production
failure_condition: When INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3%, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100K new jobs per month signals weakening labor market momentum and rising recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132,926K in September 2010 reflects ongoing post-crisis hiring
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deceleration or early recession warning
failure_condition: When PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100K, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the negative direction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with accommodative federal funds rate signals policy lag and future tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days as market anticipates rate increases and margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 192.0 in August 2010 with FEDFUNDS at 2.63%
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation pressures
failure_condition: When both inflation and accommodative rate conditions are met, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient magnitude is less than 0.15 in rolling 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly at levels below 2.5% annualized signals insufficient aggregate demand momentum and below-trend economic expansion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 10,994.3B in Q2 2010 reflects post-crisis recovery pace
regime_hint: sub-trend demand growth or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: When GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining month-over-month while retail sales remain flat signals demand-side weakness concentrated in housing and consumer discretionary sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as cyclical growth expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change < -2% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2,195K and RSAFS at 339,299M in August-September 2010
regime_hint: demand-side weakness with synchronized housing and retail deceleration
failure_condition: When both housing decline and retail stagnation conditions are met, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 52% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.
